message:
feat(scheduler): Local task storage + local wakers for thread-affine scheduling

Adds thread-local task storage support to the three-lane worker's
poll_task path, enabling tasks to be stored and retrieved from
thread-local storage (TLS) instead of the global state lock:

- poll_task now probes global storage first, falls back to TLS via
  crate::runtime::local::remove_local_task
- Introduces AnyStoredTask::Global/Local enum to track provenance
- Local tasks get dedicated ThreeLaneLocalWaker and
  ThreeLaneLocalCancelWaker that schedule to local PriorityScheduler
  instead of the global injector, reducing cross-thread contention
- On Poll::Pending, local tasks are stored back to TLS; global tasks
  go back to the global state as before
- Waker caching still uses the global task record for both variants
- task_completed waiter injection remains global (documented limitation:
  local tasks waiting on other tasks may be woken globally, consistent
  with Phase 1 Global Injector design)

This is groundwork for Phase 1 multi-threaded scheduling where
thread-affine tasks avoid the global lock for storage and wake.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
